target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $364M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C chip and semiconductor company that has raised $364M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 6 private peers at the same or adjacent stage, including d-Matrix ($2.0B), Tenstorrent ($2.7B), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), and Etched ($5.0B), alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (NVDA, AMD, ALAB).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $364M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $619M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $2.4B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.3x across the three tickers) is not applicable, as Wiliot's revenue is not disclosed.

These methods triangulate to a range of $526M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.5B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around revenue scale and market traction relative to the peer set. Stage-progression suggests a floor grounded in capital deployed; peer medians reflect market pricing for later-stage competitors with greater clarity on commercialization. Key uncertainties include addressable market penetration, manufacturing partnerships, and relative technology differentiation versus the named competitors.
